articles.title: CZ wants to make the U.S. the 'capital of crypto': State of Crypto
articles.description: The Binance founder shared his views on a variety of topics in an exclusive interview with CoinDesk earlier this month.

articles.title: Ford rehires ‘gray beard’ engineers after AI falls short
articles.description: "Mistakenly we thought that by just introducing artificial intelligence ... that would produce a high-quality product.”

articles.title: BIS warns stablecoins risk fragmenting global financial system
articles.description: The Basel-based institution said that private digital tokens fall short of the requirements for sound money and urged policymakers to accelerate work on tokenized forms of central bank and commercial bank money.

articles.title: Writer Ian Bogost says ‘The Small Stuff’ can help us reclaim our lives from too much convenience
articles.description: Has Silicon Valley been building the wrong things?

articles.title: Will Bitcoin price recover in July?
articles.description: Bitcoin’s past July gains and heavy short bets point to a rally toward $75,000, but a break below key support keeps the risk of $55,000 in play.

articles.title: EU watchdog EBA details big crypto fines as landmark laws bite
articles.description: The European Banking Authority laid out a proposed penalty framework on Friday that can strip non-compliant significant token issuers of up to 12.5% of their annual revenue.

articles.title: TechCrunch Mobility: All eyes on Tesla FSD
articles.description: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your hub for the future of transportation and now, more than ever, how AI is playing a part.

articles.title: Samson Mow says bitcoin bottom is in despite skepticism from analysts
articles.description: The bitcoin advocate said his crypto bottom claim is based on the fact that the traditional four-year halving cycle has changed, although several analysts continue to expect further downside.

articles.title: Michael Saylor teases more bitcoin buying even as Strategy stock continues to fall
articles.description: The crypto bull shrugged off market skepticism by sharing a chart of the company's $50 billion stash and teasing that they are going to need more data points.

articles.title: SBI's $289 million Bitbank deal is symptomatic of Japan's crypto consolidation: Architect Partners
articles.description: The investment bank said the purchase of Japanese crypto exchange Bitbank is a bet on regulated scale as sweeping reforms reshape the country's digital asset market.

articles.title: Why Wall Street thinks US memory maker Micron is the next Nvidia
articles.description: Eager to find more public AI-related companies that may do as well as Nvidia, Wall Street investors think they've found a winner with Micron.

articles.title: Govee’s smart nugget ice maker makes every iced drink feel like a luxury
articles.description: For some people, the ice in a beverage is almost as important as the drink itself. articles.title: Crypto's next frontier isn't crypto, it's financing AI and robotics, Framework's Anderson says
articles.description: Blockchain is becoming the financial layer for capital-intensive industries rather than just crypto-native speculation, Michael Anderson, the crypto venture firm's cofounder, said.

articles.title: Binance posts over $400M in weekly net outflows as MiCA deadline nears
articles.description: Early exchange flow data show no clear signs of a mass migration from Binance ahead of the EU MiCA transition deadline, despite rivals' efforts to attract users.

articles.title: Grayscale's Pandl hopes Strategy sells $3B in Bitcoin to restore confidence
articles.description: Grayscale's research head Zach Pandl said Strategy should sell $3 billion in Bitcoin to cover its cash obligations, but CryptoQuant argued the company has other ways to support STRC.

articles.title: Bitcoin falls below $60,000, on track for a rare back-to-back quarterly loss
articles.description: The token is down nearly 7% on the week with the altcoins falling harder. Bitcoin and ether are both ending the second quarter in the red, a back-to-back losing first half that runs against the usual pattern.
